Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Firearms Act and the Criminal Code and to make consequential changes to other Acts
Identification.ShortTitle: Strengthening Canadiansâ Security and Promoting Hunting and Recreational Shooting Act
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Firearms Act and the Criminal Code and to make consequential changes to other Acts

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Firearms Act to tighten the rules governing the transportation of firearms that are not hunting firearms. It also replaces the concept of firearms registration with that of firearms inscription and restricts the locations where firearms subject to an inscription certificate must be kept.

It also amends the Criminal Code to make changes to the definitions relating to firearms, by defining hunting firearm, amending the definition prohibited firearm and replacing the definition restricted firearm with that of circumscribed firearm.
Finally, it makes consequential changes to other Acts.

Heading.TitleText: An Act to amend the Firearms Act and the Criminal Code and to make consequential changes to other Acts

Text.Emphasis: Strengthening Canadiansâ Security and Promoting Hunting and Recreational Shooting Act

TableOfProvisions.Text: Remittance or storage of circumscribed firearm or prohibited firearm

Provision.Text: Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Strengthening Canadiansâ Security and Promoting Hunting and Recreational Shooting Act.
Subsection 2(2) of the Firearms Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: To be interpreted with Criminal Code
Subsection.Text: Unless otherwise provided, words and expressions used in this Act have the meanings assigned to them by section 2 or 84 of the Criminal Code.

Unless otherwise provided, words and expressions used in this Act have the meanings assigned to them by section 2 or 84 of the Criminal Code. Subsections 117.15(3) and (4) of that Act apply to those words and expressions.

Section.Text: Subparagraph 4(a)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: licences for firearms and authorizations and inscription certificates for prohibited firearms or circumscribed firearms, under which persons may possess firearms in circumstances that would otherwise constitute an offence under subsection 91(1), 92(1), 93(1) or 95(1) of the Criminal Code,

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 3:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of relevant portion of section 4:

Section.Text: The purpose of this Act is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to provide, notably by sections 5 to 16 and 54 to 73, for the issuance of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: licences for firearms and authorizations and registration certificates for prohibited firearms or restricted firearms, under which persons may possess firearms in circumstances that would otherwise constitute an offence under subsection 91(1), 92(1), 93(1) or 95(1) of the Criminal Code,

Section.Text: Subsection 5(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (2), in determining whether a non-resident who is 18 years old or older and by or on behalf of whom an application is made for a 60-day licence authorizing the non-resident to possess hunting firearms is eligible to hold a licence under subsection (1), a chief firearms officer or, on a reference under section 74, a provincial court judge may but need not have regard to the criteria described in subsection (2).

Despite subsection (2), in determining whether a non-resident who is 18 years old or older and by or on behalf of whom an application is made for a 60-day licence authorizing the non-resident to possess non-restricted firearms is eligible to hold a licence under subsection (1), a chief firearms officer or, on a reference under section 74, a provincial court judge may but need not have regard to the criteria described in subsection (2).
The portion of subsection 7(2) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:
An individual is eligible to hold a licence authorizing the individual to possess prohibited firearms or circumscribed firearms only if the individual

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: successfully completes a circumscribed firearms safety course that is approved by the federal Minister, as given by an instructor who is designated by a chief firearms officer, and passes any tests, as administered by an instructor who is designated by a chief firearms officer, that form part of that course;

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 5: (1)
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of relevant portions of subsection 7(2):

Subsection.Text: An individual is eligible to hold a licence authorizing the individual to possess prohibited firearms or restricted firearms only if the individual

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: successfully completes a restricted firearms safety course that is approved by the federal Minister, as given by an instructor who is designated by a chief firearms officer, and passes any tests, as administered by an instructor who is designated by a chief firearms officer, that form part of that course;

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 7(3)(b) of the Act before subparagraph (ii) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is eligible to hold a licence authorizing the individual to possess circumscribed firearms only if the individual has, after the expiration of the prohibition order,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: successfully completed a circumscribed firearms safety course that is approved by the federal Minister, as given by an instructor who is designated by a chief firearms officer, and

ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of relevant portions of subsection 7(3):

Subsection.Text: An individual against whom a prohibition order was made

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is eligible to hold a licence authorizing the individual to possess restricted firearms only if the individual has, after the expiration of the prohibition order,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: successfully completed a restricted firearms safety course that is approved by the federal Minister, as given by an instructor who is designated by a chief firearms officer, and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 7(4)(e) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: is a non-resident who is 18 years old or older and by or on behalf of whom an application is made for a 60-day licence authorizing the non-resident to possess hunting firearms.

ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of relevant portions of subsection 7(4):

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) do not apply to an individual who

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: is a non-resident who is 18 years old or older and by or on behalf of whom an application is made for a 60-day licence authorizing the non-resident to possess non-restricted firearms.

Section.Text: Subsection 8(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An individual who is less than eighteen years old is not eligible to hold a licence authorizing the individual to possess prohibited firearms or circumscribed firearms or to acquire firearms or cross-bows.

An individual who is less than eighteen years old is not eligible to hold a licence authorizing the individual to possess prohibited firearms or restricted firearms or to acquire firearms or cross-bows.
Subsection 9(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

A business is eligible to hold a licence authorizing a particular activity only if every person who stands in a prescribed relationship to the business is eligible under sections 5 and 6 to hold a licence authorizing that activity or the acquisition of circumscribed firearms.

A business is eligible to hold a licence authorizing a particular activity only if every person who stands in a prescribed relationship to the business is eligible under sections 5 and 6 to hold a licence authorizing that activity or the acquisition of restricted firearms.
Subsections 9(3) and (3.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subject to subsection (3.1), a business other than a carrier is eligible to hold a licence that authorizes the possession of firearms only if every employee of the business who, in the course of duties of employment, handles or would handle firearms is the holder of a licence authorizing the holder to acquire hunting firearms.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Employees â prohibited firearms or circumscribed firearms
Subsection.Text: A business other than a carrier is eligible to hold a licence that authorizes the possession of prohibited firearms or circumscribed firearms only if every employee of the business who, in the course of duties of employment, handles or would handle firearms is the holder of a licence authorizing the holder to acquire circumscribed firearms.

Subject to subsection (3.1), a business other than a carrier is eligible to hold a licence that authorizes the possession of firearms only if every employee of the business who, in the course of duties of employment, handles or would handle firearms is the holder of a licence authorizing the holder to acquire non-restricted firearms.

A business other than a carrier is eligible to hold a licence that authorizes the possession of prohibited firearms or restricted firearms only if every employee of the business who, in the course of duties of employment, handles or would handle firearms is the holder of a licence authorizing the holder to acquire restricted firearms.
Paragraph 12(2)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: beginning on the commencement day was continuously the holder of an inscription certificate for one or more automatic firearms.

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 8: (1)
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of relevant portions of subsection 12(2):

Subsection.Text: An individual is eligible to hold a licence authorizing the individual to possess automatic firearms that, on the commencement day, were registered as restricted weapons under the former Act if the individual

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: beginning on the commencement day was continuously the holder of a registration certificate for one or more automatic firearms.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 12(3)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: beginning on the commencement day was continuously the holder of an inscription certificate for one or more automatic firearms that have been so altered.

ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of relevant portions of subsection 12(3):

Subsection.Text: An individual is eligible to hold a licence authorizing the individual to possess automatic firearms that have been altered to discharge only one projectile during one pressure of the trigger and that, on the commencement day, were registered as restricted weapons under the former Act if the individual

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: beginning on the commencement day was continuously the holder of a registration certificate for one or more automatic firearms that have been so altered.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 12(4)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: beginning on the commencement day was continuously the holder of an inscription certificate for one or more firearms that were so declared.

ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of relevant portions of subsection 12(4):

Subsection.Text: An individual is eligible to hold a licence authorizing the individual to possess firearms that were declared to be prohibited weapons under the former Act by the Prohibited Weapons Order, No. 12, made by Order in Council P.C. 1992-1690 of July 23, 1992 and registered as SOR/92-471 and that, on October 1, 1992, either were registered as restricted weapons under the former Act or were the subject of an application for a registration certificate under the former Act if the individual

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: beginning on the commencement day was continuously the holder of a registration certificate for one or more firearms that were so declared.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 12(5)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: beginning on the commencement day was continuously the holder of an inscription certificate for one or more firearms that were so declared.

ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of relevant portions of subsection 12(5):

Subsection.Text: An individual is eligible to hold a licence authorizing the individual to possess firearms that were declared to be prohibited weapons under the former Act by the Prohibited Weapons Order, No. 13, made by Order in Council P.C. 1994-1974 of November 29, 1994 and registered as SOR/94-741 and that, on January 1, 1995, either were registered as restricted weapons under the former Act or were the subject of an application for a registration certificate under the former Act if the individual

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: beginning on the commencement day was continuously the holder of a registration certificate for one or more firearms that were so declared.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 12(6)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: beginning on December 1, 1998, the particular individual was continuously the holder of an inscription certificate for that kind of handgun.

ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of relevant portions of subsection 12(6):

Subsection.Text: A particular individual is eligible to hold a licence authorizing that particular individual to possess a handgun referred to in subsection (6.1) if

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: beginning on December 1, 1998 the particular individual was continuously the holder of a registration certificate for that kind of handgun.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 12(8) of the Act after subparagraph (b)(ii) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedParagraph.Text: was continuously the holder of an inscription certificate for one or more of those firearms.

ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of relevant portions of subsection 12(8):

Subsection.Text: An individual is, in the prescribed circumstances, eligible to hold a licence authorizing the individual to possess firearms prescribed by a provision of regulations made by the Governor in Council under section 117.15 of the Criminal Code to be prohibited firearms if the individual

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: beginning on

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an individual who on that day did not hold but had applied for a registration certificate for one or more of those firearms, the day on which the registration certificate was issued

ContinuedParagraph.Text: was continuously the holder of a registration certificate for one or more of those firearms.

Subsection.Text: Section 12 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (8):

For the purposes of paragraphs 12(2)(c), (3)(c), (4)(c), (5)(c), (6)(b) and 8(b), inscription certificate includes, in respect of any firearm or handgun referred to in these paragraphs, a registration certificate under this Act as it read before the coming into force of this subsection.

Section.Text: The heading before section 12.1 and sections 12.1 and 13 of the Act are replaced by the following:
An inscription certificate may only be issued for a prohibited firearm or a circumscribed firearm.
A person is not eligible to hold an inscription certificate for a firearm unless the person holds a licence authorizing the person to possess that kind of firearm.

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 9:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of the heading and sections 12.1 and 13:

Section.Text: A registration certificate may only be issued for a prohibited firearm or a restricted firearm.
A person is not eligible to hold a registration certificate for a firearm unless the person holds a licence authorizing the person to possess that kind of firearm.
The portion of section 14 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
An inscription certificate may be issued only for a firearm

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 10:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of relevant portion of section 14:

Section.Text: A registration certificate may be issued only for a firearm
Section 15 of the Act is replaced by the following:
An inscription certificate may not be issued for a firearm that is owned by Her Majesty in right of Canada or a province or by a police force.
A registration certificate may not be issued for a firearm that is owned by Her Majesty in right of Canada or a province or by a police force.
Subsection 16(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Only one person per inscription certificate

Subsection.Text: An inscription certificate for a firearm may be issued to only one person.
A registration certificate for a firearm may be issued to only one person.

Section.Text: Section 17 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Places where prohibited and circumscribed firearms may be possessed

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2) and to sections 19 and 20, a prohibited firearm or circumscribed firearm, the holder of the inscription certificate for which is an individual, may be possessed only at a shooting club or a storage facility recorded in the Canadian Firearms Registry.

A handgun referred to in subsection 12(6.1) (pre-December 1, 1998 handguns) or a circumscribed firearm, the holder of the inscription certificate for which is an individual, may also be possessed at the dwelling-house of the individual, as recorded in the Canadian Firearms Registry, if the individual possesses it in that dwelling-house as part of a collection and fulfills the conditions set out in section 30.

Section.Text: Subject to sections 19 and 20, a prohibited firearm or restricted firearm, the holder of the registration certificate for which is an individual, may be possessed only at the dwelling-house of the individual, as recorded in the Canadian Firearms Registry, or at a place authorized by a chief firearms officer.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 19(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Reasons â transporting and using prohibited firearms or circumscribed firearms

Subsection.Text: An individual who holds a licence authorizing the individual to possess prohibited firearms or circumscribed firearms may not be authorized to cause a particular prohibited firearm or circumscribed firearm to be transported between two or more specified places except for the following reasons:

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 14: (1) and (2)
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of relevant portions of subsection 19(1):

Subsection.Text: An individual who holds a licence authorizing the individual to possess prohibited firearms or restricted firearms may be authorized to transport a particular prohibited firearm or restricted firearm between two or more specified places for any good and sufficient reason, including, without restricting the generality of the foregoing,

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: to provide instructions in the use of firearms as part of a restricted firearms safety course that is approved by the federal Minister; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the individual

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: changes residence,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: wishes to transport the firearm to a peace officer, firearms officer or chief firearms officer for registration or disposal in accordance with this Act or Part III of the Criminal Code,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: wishes to transport the firearm for repair, storage, sale, exportation or appraisal, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: wishes to transport the firearm to a gun show.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 19(1)(a.1) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: to provide instructions in the use of firearms as part of a circumscribed firearms safety course that is approved by the federal Minister; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the individual

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: changes residence while he or she possesses a handgun referred to in subsection 12(6.1) (pre-December 1, 1998 handguns) as part of a collection in accordance with section 30,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: wishes to present the prohibited firearm or circumscribed firearm to a peace officer, firearms officer or chief firearms officer for inscription or disposal in accordance with this Act or Part III of the Criminal Code,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: wishes to cause the prohibited firearm or circumscribed firearm to be transported for repair, storage, sale, exportation or appraisal, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: wishes to cause the prohibited firearm or circumscribed firearm to be transported to a gun show.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 19(2) to (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exception for prohibited firearms other than prohibited handguns
Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), an individual must not be authorized to cause a prohibited firearm, other than a handgun referred to in subsection 12(6.1) (pre-December 1, 1998) to be transported between specified places except for the purposes referred to in paragraph (1)(b).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Automatic authorization to transport â licence renewal
Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2.3), an individual who holds a licence authorizing the individual to possess prohibited firearms or circumscribed firearms must, if the licence is renewed, be authorized to cause them to be transported within the individualâs province of residence

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to and from all shooting clubs and shooting ranges that are approved under section 29;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to and from any place a peace officer, firearms officer or chief firearms officer is located, for verification, inscription or disposal in accordance with this Act or Part III of the Criminal Code;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to and from a business that holds a licence authorizing it to repair or appraise prohibited firearms or circumscribed firearms;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: to and from a gun show; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: to a port of exit in order to take them outside Canada, and from a port of entry.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Automatic authorization to transport â transfer
Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2.3), if a chief firearms officer has authorized the transfer of a prohibited firearm or a circumscribed firearm to an individual who holds a licence authorizing the individual to possess prohibited firearms or circumscribed firearms, the individual must be authorized

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to cause the firearm to be transported within the individualâs province of residence from the place where the individual acquires it to the place where they may possess it under section 17; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to cause their prohibited firearms and circumscribed firearms to be transported within the individualâs province of residence to and from the places referred to in any of paragraphs (2.1)(a) to (e).

Subsection.Text: An individual must not be authorized under subsection (2.1) or (2.2) to cause to be transported the following firearms to or from the places referred to in paragraph (2.1)(a):

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a prohibited firearm, other than a handgun referred to in subsection 12(6.1) (pre-December 1, 1998); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a circumscribed firearm or a handgun referred to in subsection 12(6.1) (pre-December 1, 1998) whose transfer was approved, in accordance with subparagraph 28(b)(ii), for the purpose of forming part of a gun collection.

Subsection.Text: A non-resident may be authorized to cause a particular circumscribed firearm to be transported between specified places in accordance with sections 35 and 35.1.

Despite subsection (1), an individual must not be authorized to transport a prohibited firearm, other than a handgun referred to in subsection 12(6.1), between specified places except for the purposes referred to in paragraph (1)(b).

Subject to subsection (2.3), an individÂ­ual who holds a licence authorizing the individual to possess prohibited firearms or restricted firearms must, if the licence is renewed, be authorized to transport them within the individualâs province of residence

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to and from all shooting clubs and shooting ranges that are approved under section 29;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to and from any place a peace officer, firearms officer or chief firearms officer is located, for verification, registration or disposal in accordance with this Act or Part III of the Criminal Code;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to and from a business that holds a licence authorizing it to repair or appraise prohibited firearms or restricted firearms;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: to and from a gun show; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: to a port of exit in order to take them outside Canada, and from a port of entry.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2.3), if a chief firearms officer has authorized the transfer of a prohibited firearm or a restricted firearm to an individual who holds a licence authorizing the individual to possess prohibited firearms or restricted firearms, the individual must be authorized

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to transport the firearm within the individualâs province of residence from the place where the individual acquires it to the place where they may possess it under section 17; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to transport their prohibited firearms and restricted firearms within the individualâs province of residence to and from the places referred to in any of paragraphs (2.1)(a) to (e).

Subsection.Text: An individual must not be authorized under subsection (2.1) or (2.2) to transport the following firearms to or from the places referred to in paragraph (2.1)(a):

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a prohibited firearm, other than a handgun referred to in subsection 12(6.1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a restricted firearm or a handgun referred to in subsection 12(6.1) whose transfer was approved, in accordance with subparagraph 28(b)(ii), for the purpose of forming part of a gun collection.

Subsection.Text: A non-resident may be authorized to transport a particular restricted firearm between specified places in accordance with sections 35 and 35.1.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 19:

Section.MarginalNote: Transporting prohibited firearms or circumscribed firearms
Section.Text: Only a carrier may transport a prohibited firearm or a circumscribed firearm referred to in section 19.
Section 20 of the Act is replaced by the following:

An individual who holds a licence authorizing the individual to possess circumscribed firearms or handguns referred to in subsection 12(6.1) (pre-December 1, 1998 handguns) may be authorized to possess a particular circumscribed firearm or handgun at a place other than the place at which it is authorized to be possessed if the individual needs the particular circumscribed firearm or handgun

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to protect the life of that individual or of other individuals; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for use in connection with his or her lawful profession or occupation.

Section.Text: An individual who holds a licence authorizing the individual to possess restricted firearms or handguns referred to in subsection 12(6.1) (pre-December 1, 1998 handguns) may be authorized to possess a particular restricted firearm or handgun at a place other than the place at which it is authorized to be possessed if the individual needs the particular restricted firearm or handgun

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to protect the life of that individual or of other individuals; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for use in connection with his or her lawful profession or occupation.

Section.Text: The portion of section 23 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
A person may transfer a hunting firearm if, at the time of the transfer,

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 17:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of relevant portion of section 23:

Section.Text: A person may transfer a non-restricted firearm if, at the time of the transfer,

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 23.2(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Authorization to transfer prohibited or circumscribed firearms

Subsection.Text: A person may transfer a prohibited firearm or a circumscribed firearm if, at the time of the transfer,

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 18: (1) and (2)
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of relevant portions of subsection 23.2(1):

Subsection.Text: A person may transfer a prohibited firearm or a restricted firearm if, at the time of the transfer,

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a new registration certificate for the firearm is issued in accordance with this Act; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 23.2(1)(e) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a new inscription certificate for the firearm is issued in accordance with this Act; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 23.2(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If, after being informed of a proposed transfer of a firearm, the Registrar decides to refuse to issue an inscription certificate for the firearm, the Registrar shall inform a chief firearms officer of that decision.

If, after being informed of a proposed transfer of a firearm, the Registrar decides to refuse to issue a registration certificate for the firearm, the Registrar shall inform a chief firearms officer of that decision.

Section.Text: Subsection 26(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Authorization to transfer prohibited or circumscribed firearms to Crown, etc.

Subsection.Text: A person may transfer a prohibited firearm or a circumscribed firearm to Her Majesty in right of Canada or a province, to a police force or to a municipality if the person informs the Registrar of the transfer and complies with the prescribed conditions.

A person may transfer a prohibited firearm or a restricted firearm to Her Majesty in right of Canada or a province, to a police force or to a municipality if the person informs the Registrar of the transfer and complies with the prescribed conditions.
The portion of section 27 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: On being informed of a proposed transfer of a prohibited firearm or circumscribed firearm under section 23.2, a chief firearms officer shall

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 20: (1) and (2)
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of relevant portions of section 27:

Section.Text: On being informed of a proposed transfer of a prohibited firearm or restricted firearm under section 23.2, a chief firearms officer shall

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a proposed transfer of a restricted firearm or a handgun referred to in subsection 12(6.1) (pre-December 1, 1998 handguns), verify the purpose for which the transferee or individual wishes to acquire the restricted firearm or handgun and determine whether the particular restricted firearm or handgun is appropriate for that purpose;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 27(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a proposed transfer of a circumscribed firearm or a handgun referred to in subsection 12(6.1) (pre-December 1, 1998 handguns), verify the purpose for which the transferee or individual wishes to acquire the circumscribed firearm or handgun and determine whether the particular circumscribed firearm or handgun is appropriate for that purpose;

Subsection.Text: The portion of section 28 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: A chief firearms officer may approve the transfer to an individual of a circumscribed firearm or a handgun referred to in subsection 12(6.1) (pre-December 1, 1998 handguns) only if the chief firearms officer is satisfied

A chief firearms officer may approve the transfer to an individual of a restricted firearm or a handgun referred to in subsection 12(6.1) (pre-December 1, 1998 handguns) only if the chief firearms officer is satisfied

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that the individual needs the restricted firearm or handgun

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to protect the life of that individual or of other individuals, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for use in connection with his or her lawful profession or occupation; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that the purpose for which the individual wishes to acquire the restricted firearm or handgun is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: for use in target practice, or a target shooting competition, under conditions specified in an authorization to transport or under the auspices of a shooting club or shooting range that is approved under section 29, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to form part of a gun collection of the individual, in the case of an individual who satisfies the criteria described in section 30.

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 28(a) of the English version of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that the individual needs the circumscribed firearm or handgun

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 28(b) of the English version of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that the purpose for which the individual wishes to acquire the circumscribed firearm or handgun is

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 30(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: has knowledge of the historical, technological or scientific characteristics that relate to or distinguish the circumscribed firearms or handguns referred to in subsection 12(6.1) (pre-December 1, 1998 handguns) that he or she possesses;

Section.Text: The criteria referred to in subparagraph 28(b)(ii) are that the individual

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: has knowledge of the historical, technological or scientific characteristics that relate or distinguish the restricted firearms or handguns that he or she possesses;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has consented to the periodic inspection, conducted in a reasonable manner, of the premises in which the restricted firearms or handguns are to be kept; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: has complied with such other requirements as are prescribed respecting knowledge, secure storage and the keeping of records in respect of restricted firearms or handguns.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 30(b) and (c) of the English version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: has complied with such other requirements as are prescribed respecting knowledge, secure storage and the keeping of records in respect of circumscribed firearms or handguns.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 31(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)

Paragraph.Label: (b)

Subsection.Text: On being informed of a proposed transfer of a firearm, the Registrar may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: issue a new registration certificate for the firearm in accordance with this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: revoke any registration certificate for the firearm held by the transferor.

Subsection.Text: On being informed of a transfer of a firearm to Her Majesty in right of Canada or a province or to a police force, the Registrar shall revoke any registration certificate for the firearm.
Subsection 31(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Transfers of firearms to the Crown and to the police
Subsection.Text: On being informed of a transfer of a firearm to Her Majesty in right of Canada or a province or to a police force, the Registrar shall revoke any inscription certificate for the firearm.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 35(1)(a)(iii) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a circumscribed firearm, produces an authorization to transport the circumscribed firearm; and

Subsection.Text: A non-resident who does not hold a licence may import a firearm that is not a prohibited firearm if, at the time of the importation,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the non-resident

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is eighteen years old or older,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: declares the firearm to a customs officer in the prescribed manner and, in the case of a declaration in writing, completes the prescribed form containing the prescribed information, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a restricted firearm, produces an authorization to transport the restricted firearm; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a customs officer confirms in the prescribed manner the declaration referred to in subparagraph (a)(ii) and the authorization to transport referred to in subparagraph (a)(iii).

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 35(1)(c) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a hunting firearm is declared at a customs office to a customs officer and

Subsection.Text: If a non-restricted firearm is declared at a customs office to a customs officer and

Section.Text: Subsection 36(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A declaration that is confirmed under paragraph 35(1)(b) has the same effect after the importation of the firearm as a licence authorizing the non-resident to possess only that firearm and, in the case of a circumscribed firearm, as an inscription certificate for the firearm until

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the expiry of 60 days after the importation, in the case of a hunting firearm; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the earlier of the expiry of 60 days after the importation and the expiry of the authorization to transport, in the case of a circumscribed firearm.

Subsection.Text: A declaration that is confirmed under paragraph 35(1)(b) has the same effect after the importation of the firearm as a licence authorizing the non-resident to possess only that firearm and, in the case of a restricted firearm, as a registration certificate for the firearm until

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the expiry of 60 days after the importation, in the case of a non-restricted firearm; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the earlier of the expiry of 60 days after the importation and the expiry of the authorization to transport, in the case of a restricted firearm.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following in numerical order:

A business may import a prohibited firearm or a circumscribed firearm only if the business completes the prescribed form containing the prescribed information and provides it by electronic or other means to the Registrar before the importation and to a customs officer before or at the time of the importation.
The heading âLicences, Registration Certificates and Authorizationsâ before section 54 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subsection 54(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

A licence, inscription certificate or authorization, other than an authorization referred to in subsection 19(2.1) or (2.2), may be issued only on application made in the prescribed form â which form may be in writing or electronic â or in the prescribed manner. The application must set out the prescribed information and be accompanied by payment of the prescribed fees.

A licence, registration certificate or authorization, other than an authorization referred to in subsection 19(2.1) or (2.2), may be issued only on application made in the prescribed form â which form may be in writing or electronic â or in the prescribed manner. The application must set out the prescribed information and be accompanied by payment of the prescribed fees.

Paragraph.Label: (a)

Section.Text: Section 59 of the Act is replaced by the following:

An individual who holds an authorization to carry or authorization to transport need not be the person to whom the inscription certificate for the particular prohibited firearm or circumscribed firearm was issued.

An individual who holds an authorization to carry or authorization to transport need not be the person to whom the registration certificate for the particular prohibited firearm or restricted firearm was issued.
Section 60 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Inscription certificates and authorizations to export or import
Section.Text: The Registrar is responsible for issuing inscription certificates for prohibited firearms and circumscribed firearms and assigning firearms identification numbers to them and for issuing authorizations to export and authorizations to import.

The Registrar is responsible for issuing registration certificates for prohibited firearms and restricted firearms and assigning firearms identification numbers to them and for issuing authorizations to export and authorizations to import.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 61(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

A licence or inscription certificate must be issued in the prescribed form â which form may be in writing or electronic â or in the prescribed manner, and include the prescribed information, including any conditions attached to it.

A licence or registration certificate must be issued in the prescribed form â which form may be in writing or electronic â or in the prescribed manner, and include the prescribed information, including any conditions attached to it.

Section.Text: Section 62 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Licences, inscription certificates, authorizations to carry, authorizations to transport, authorizations to export and authorizations to import are not transferable.
Licences, registration certificates, authorizations to carry, authorizations to transport, authorizations to export and authorizations to import are not transferable.
Subsection 63(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Licences, inscription certificates, authorizations to transport, authorizations to export and authorizations to import are valid throughout Canada.
Licences, registration certificates, authorizations to transport, authorizations to export and authorizations to import are valid throughout Canada.

An inscription certificate for a prohibited firearm or a circumscribed firearm expires when

Subsection.Text: Subsection 67(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

On renewing a licence authorizing an individual to possess circumscribed firearms or handguns referred to in subsection 12(6.1) (pre-December 1, 1998 handguns), a chief firearms officer shall decide whether any of those firearms or handguns that the individual possesses are being used for a purpose described in section 28.

On renewing a licence authorizing an individual to possess restricted firearms or handguns referred to in subsection 12(6.1) (pre-December 1, 1998 handguns), a chief firearms officer shall decide whether any of those firearms or handguns that the individual possesses are being used for a purpose described in section 28.

A chief firearms officer who decides that any restricted firearms or any handguns referred to in subsection 12(6.1) (pre-December 1, 1998 handguns) that are possessed by an individual are not being used for that purpose shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: give notice of that decision in the prescribed form to the individual; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: inform the Registrar of that decision.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 67(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

A chief firearms officer who decides that any circumscribed firearms or any handguns referred to in subsection 12(6.1) (pre-December 1, 1998 handguns) that are possessed by an individual are not being used for that purpose shall

Section.Text: Section 69 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Registrar may refuse to issue an inscription certificate, authorization to export or authorization to import for any good and sufficient reason including, in the case of an application for an inscription certificate, where the applicant is not eligible to hold an inscription certificate.

The Registrar may refuse to issue a registration certificate, authorization to export or authorization to import for any good and sufficient reason including, in the case of an application for a registration certificate, where the applicant is not eligible to hold a registration certificate.
Section 71 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Registrar

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: may revoke an inscription certificate for a prohibited firearm or a circumscribed firearm for any good and sufficient reason; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: shall revoke an inscription certificate for a firearm held by an individual where the Registrar is informed by a chief firearms officer under section 67 that the firearm is not being used for a purpose described in section 28.

Subsection.Text: An inscription certificate for a prohibited firearm referred to in subsection 12(3) (pre-August 1, 1992 converted automatic firearms) is automatically revoked on the change of any alteration in the prohibited firearm that was described in the application for the inscription certificate.
The Registrar

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: may revoke a registration certificate for a prohibited firearm or a restricted firearm for any good and sufficient reason; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: shall revoke a registration certificate for a firearm held by an individual where the Registrar is informed by a chief firearms officer under section 67 that the firearm is not being used for a purpose described in section 28.

Subsection.Text: A registration certificate for a prohibited firearm referred to in subsection 12(3) (pre-August 1, 1992 converted automatic firearms) is automatically revoked on the change of any alteration in the prohibited firearm that was described in the application for the registration certificate.
Subsection 72(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Notice of refusal to issue or revocation

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (1.1), if a chief firearms officer decides to refuse to issue or to revoke a licence or authorization to transport or the Registrar decides to refuse to issue or to revoke an inscription certificate, authorization to export or authorization to import, the chief firearms officer or Registrar shall give notice of the decision in the prescribed form to the applicant for or holder of the licence, inscription certificate or authorization.
Paragraphs 72(1.1)(a) and (b) of the English version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the holder has requested that the licence, inscription certificate or authorization be revoked; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the revocation is incidental to the issuance of a new licence, inscription certificate or authorization.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (1.1), if a chief firearms officer decides to refuse to issue or to revoke a licence or authorization to transport or the Registrar decides to refuse to issue or to revoke a registration certificate, authorization to export or authorization to import, the chief firearms officer or Registrar shall give notice of the decision in the prescribed form to the applicant for or holder of the licence, registration certificate or authorization.
Notice under subsection (1) need not be given in any of the following circumstances:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the holder has requested that the licence, registration certificate or authorization be revoked; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the revocation is incidental to the issuance of a new licence, registration certificate or authorization.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 72(5) and (6) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Disposal of firearms â inscription certificate
Subsection.Text: A notice given under subsection (1) in respect of an inscription certificate for a prohibited firearm or a circumscribed firearm must specify a reasonable period during which the applicant for or holder of the inscription certificate may deliver to a peace officer or a firearms officer or a chief firearms officer or otherwise lawfully dispose of the firearm to which the inscription certificate relates and during which sections 91, 92 and 94 of the Criminal Code do not apply to the applicant or holder.

If the applicant for or holder of the licence or inscription certificate refers the refusal to issue it or revocation of it to a provincial court judge under section 74, the reasonable period of time does not begin until after the reference is finally disposed of.

A notice given under subsection (1) in respect of a registration certificate for a prohibited firearm or a restricted firearm must specify a reasonable period during which the applicant for or holder of the registration certificate may deliver to a peace officer or a firearms officer or a chief firearms officer or otherwise lawfully dispose of the firearm to which the registration certificate relates and during which sections 91, 92 and 94 of the Criminal Code do not apply to the applicant or holder.

If the applicant for or holder of the licence or registration certificate refers the refusal to issue it or revocation of it to a provincial court judge under section 74, the reasonable period of time does not begin until after the reference is finally disposed of.
Paragraph 74(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a chief firearms officer or the Registrar refuses to issue or revokes a licence, inscription certificate, authorization to transport, authorization to export or authorization to import,

Paragraph.Text: a chief firearms officer or the Registrar refuses to issue or revokes a licence, registration certificate, authorization to transport, authorization to export or authorization to import,

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 74(1) of the English version of the Act after paragraph (c) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the applicant for or holder of the licence, inscription certificate, authorization or approval may refer the matter to a provincial court judge in the territorial division in which the applicant or holder resides.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 75(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

On receipt of a reference under section 74, the provincial court judge shall fix a date for the hearing of the reference and direct that notice of the hearing be given to the chief firearms officer, Registrar or provincial minister and to the applicant for or holder of the licence, inscription certificate, authorization or approval, in such manner as the provincial court judge may specify.

On receipt of a reference under section 74, the provincial court judge shall fix a date for the hearing of the reference and direct that notice of the hearing be given to the chief firearms officer, Registrar or provincial minister and to the applicant for or holder of the licence, registration certificate, authorization or approval, in such manner as the provincial court judge may specify.
Subsection 75(3) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

At the hearing of the reference, the burden of proof is on the applicant or holder to satisfy the provincial court judge that the refusal to issue or revocation of the licence, inscription certificate or authorization, the decision or the refusal to approve or revocation of the approval was not justified.

At the hearing of the reference, the burden of proof is on the applicant or holder to satisfy the provincial court judge that the refusal to issue or revocation of the licence, registration certificate or authorization, the decision or the refusal to approve or revocation of the approval was not justified.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 76(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: direct the chief firearms officer or Registrar to issue a licence, inscription certificate or authorization or direct the provincial minister to approve a shooting club or shooting range; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: cancel the revocation of the licence, inscription certificate, authorization or approval or the decision of the chief firearms officer under section 67.

Paragraph.Label: (c)

Section.Text: Subsection 77(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 78, where a provincial court judge makes an order under paragraph 76(a), the applicant for or holder of the licence, inscription certificate, authorization or approval, as the case may be, may appeal to the superior court against the order.

Subject to section 78, where a provincial court judge makes an order under paragraph 76(a), the applicant for or holder of the licence, registration certificate, authorization or approval, as the case may be, may appeal to the superior court against the order.

Section.Text: The heading âCanadian Firearms Registration Systemâ before section 82 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: An individual to be known as the Registrar of Firearms shall be appointed or deployed in accordance with the Public Service Employment Act.
Paragraphs 83(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: every licence, every inscription certificate for a prohibited firearm or a circumscribed firearm and every authorization that is issued or revoked by the Registrar;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: every application for a licence, an inscription certificate for a prohibited firearm or a circumscribed firearm or an authorization that is refused by the Registrar;

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 50:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of relevant portions of subsection 83(1):

Subsection.Text: The Registrar shall establish and maintain a registry, to be known as the Canadian Firearms Registry, in which shall be kept a record of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: every licence, every registration certificate for a prohibited firearm or a restricted firearm and every authorization that is issued or revoked by the Registrar;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: every application for a licence, a registration certificate for a prohibited firearm or a restricted firearm or an authorization that is refused by the Registrar;

Section.Text: Section 88 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Reporting of loss, finding, theft and destruction
Section.Text: A chief firearms officer to whom the loss, finding, theft or destruction of a prohibited firearm or a circumscribed firearm is reported shall have the Registrar informed without delay of the loss, finding, theft or destruction.

A chief firearms officer to whom the loss, finding, theft or destruction of a prohibited firearm or a restricted firearm is reported shall have the Registrar informed without delay of the loss, finding, theft or destruction.
Paragraph 95(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: providing for payment of compensation by Canada to the provinces in respect of administrative costs actually incurred by the provinces in relation to processing licences, inscription certificates and authorizations and applications for licences, inscription certificates and authorizations and the operation of the Canadian Firearms Inscription System; and

ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of relevant portions of section 95:

Section.Text: The federal Minister may, with the approval of the Governor in Council, enter into agreements with the governments of the provinces

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: providing for payment of compensation by Canada to the provinces in respect of administrative costs actually incurred by the provinces in relation to processing licences, registration certificates and authorizations and applications for licences, registration certificates and authorizations and the operation of the Canadian Firearms Registration System; and

Section.Text: Section 96 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The issuance of a licence, inscription certificate or authorization under this Act does not affect the obligation of any person to comply with any other Act of Parliament or any regulation made under an Act of Parliament respecting firearms or other weapons.

The issuance of a licence, registration certificate or authorization under this Act does not affect the obligation of any person to comply with any other Act of Parliament or any regulation made under an Act of Parliament respecting firearms or other weapons.
Section 105 of the Act is replaced by the following:

An inspector who believes on reasonable grounds that a person possesses a firearm may, by demand made to that person, require that person, within a reasonable time after the demand is made, to produce the firearm in the manner specified by the inspector for the purpose of verifying the serial number or other identifying features of the firearm and of ensuring that, in the case of a prohibited firearm or a circumscribed firearm, the person is the holder of the inscription certificate for it.

An inspector who believes on reasonable grounds that a person possesses a firearm may, by demand made to that person, require that person, within a reasonable time after the demand is made, to produce the firearm in the manner specified by the inspector for the purpose of verifying the serial number or other identifying features of the firearm and of ensuring that, in the case of a prohibited firearm or a restricted firearm, the person is the holder of the registration certificate for it.
Subsection 106(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: False statements to procure licences, etc.

Subsection.Text: Every person commits an offence who, for the purpose of procuring a licence, inscription certificate or authorization for that person or any other person, knowingly makes a statement orally or in writing that is false or misleading or knowingly fails to disclose any information that is relevant to the application for the licence, inscription certificate or authorization.

Every person commits an offence who, for the purpose of procuring a licence, registration certificate or authorization for that person or any other person, knowingly makes a statement orally or in writing that is false or misleading or knowingly fails to disclose any information that is relevant to the application for the licence, registration certificate or authorization.

Section.Text: Paragraph 107(a) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a licence, inscription certificate or authorization; or

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 56:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of relevant portion of section 107:

Section.Text: Every person commits an offence who, without lawful excuse the proof of which lies on the person, alters, defaces or falsifies

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a licence, registration certificate or authorization; or

Section.Text: Section 110 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Contravention of conditions of licences, etc.
Section.Text: Every person commits an offence who, without lawful excuse, contravenes a condition of a licence, inscription certificate or authorization held by the person.
Every person commits an offence who, without lawful excuse, contravenes a condition of a licence, registration certificate or authorization held by the person.
Section 114 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Failure to deliver up revoked licence, etc.
Section.Text: Every person commits an offence who, being the holder of a licence, an inscription certificate for a prohibited firearm or a circumscribed firearm or an authorization that is revoked, does not deliver it up to a peace officer or firearms officer without delay after the revocation.

Every person commits an offence who, being the holder of a licence, a registration certificate for a prohibited firearm or a restricted firearm or an authorization that is revoked, does not deliver it up to a peace officer or firearms officer without delay after the revocation.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 117(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: regulating the issuance of licences, inscription certificates and authorizations, including regulations respecting the purposes for which they may be issued under any provision of this Act and prescribing the circumstances in which persons are or are not eligible to hold licences;

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations

Section.Text: Subsection 121(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A permit that is deemed to be a licence authorizes the holder to possess hunting firearms.
A permit that is deemed to be a licence authorizes the holder to possess non-restricted firearms.

Section.Text: The Act is modified by adding, after section 135, the following:

Subsection.Text: A comprehensive review of the provisions and operation of this Act must be undertaken, every five years after this section comes into force, by the committee of the Senate, of the House of Commons, or of both Houses of Parliament, that may be designated or established for that purpose.

The committee referred to in subsection (1) must, within a year after the review is undertaken or within any further period that the Senate, the House of Commons, or both Houses of Parliament, as the case may be, may authorize, submit a report on the review to Parliament that includes a statement of any changes to this Act or its operation that the committee recommends.

Section.Text: Section 2.1 of the Criminal Code is replaced by the following:

In this Act, ammunition, antique firearm, automatic firearm, cartridge magazine, circumscribed firearm, cross-bow, handgun, hunting firearm, imitation firearm, prohibited ammunition, prohibited device, prohibited firearm, prohibited weapon, replica firearm and restricted weapon, as well as authorization, inscription certificate and licence when used in relation to those words and expressions, have the same meaning as in subsection 84(1).

In this Act, ammunition, antique firearm, automatic firearm, cartridge magazine, cross-bow, handgun, imitation firearm, prohibited ammunition, prohibited device, prohibited firearm, prohibited weapon, replica firearm, restricted firearm and restricted weapon, as well as authorization, licence and registration certificate when used in relation to those words and expressions, have the same meaning as in subsection 84(1).
Paragraph 83.3(11)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the authorizations, licences and inscription certificates that are held by the person shall be surrendered.

Paragraph.Label: (b)

Section.Text: Subsection 107(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every person commits an offence who knowingly makes, before a peace officer, firearms officer or chief firearms officer, a false report or statement concerning the loss, theft or destruction of a prohibited firearm, a circumscribed firearm, a hunting firearm, a prohibited weapon, a restricted weapon, a prohibited device, any prohibited ammunition, an authorization, a licence or an inscription certificate.

Every person commits an offence who knowingly makes, before a peace officer, firearms officer or chief firearms officer, a false report or statement concerning the loss, theft or destruction of a prohibited firearm, a restricted firearm, a non-restricted firearm, a prohibited weapon, a restricted weapon, a prohibited device, any prohibited ammunition, an authorization, a licence or a registration certificate.

Section.Text: Paragraph 108(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: No person is guilty of an offence under paragraph (1)(b) by reason only of possessing a prohibited firearm or circumscribed firearm the serial number on which has been altered, defaced or removed, if that serial number has been replaced and an inscription certificate in respect of the firearm has been issued setting out a new serial number for the firearm.

No person is guilty of an offence under paragraph (1)(b) by reason only of possessing a prohibited firearm or restricted firearm the serial number on which has been altered, defaced or removed, if that serial number has been replaced and a registration certificate in respect of the firearm has been issued setting out a new serial number for the firearm.

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any prohibited firearm, circumscribed firearm, prohibited weapon, prohibited device and prohibited ammunition for life.

Subsection.Text: An order made under subsection (1) shall, in the case of a first conviction for or discharge from the offence to which the order relates, prohibit the person from possessing

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any firearm, other than a prohibited firearm or restricted firearm, and any crossbow, restricted weapon, ammunition and explosive substance during the period that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: begins on the day on which the order is made, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: ends not earlier than ten years after the personâs release from imprisonment after conviction for the offence or, if the person is not then imprisoned or subject to imprisonment, after the personâs conviction for or discharge from the offence; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any prohibited firearm, restricted firearm, prohibited weapon, prohibited device and prohibited ammunition for life.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 113(1) of the French version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Text.Language: La juridiction compÃ©tente peut rendre une ordonnance autorisant le contrÃ´leur des armes Ã feu ou le directeur Ã dÃ©livrer Ã une personne qui est ou sera visÃ©e par une ordonnance dâinterdiction, une autorisation, un permis ou un certificat dâimmatriculation, selon le cas, aux conditions quâelle estime indiquÃ©es, si cette personne la convainc :

Subsection.Text: Where a person who is or will be a person against whom a prohibition order is made establishes to the satisfaction of a competent authority that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person needs a firearm or restricted weapon to hunt or trap in order to sustain the person or the personâs family, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a prohibition order against the person would constitute a virtual prohibition against employment in the only vocation open to the person,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the competent authority may, notwithstanding that the person is or will be subject to a prohibition order, make an order authorizing a chief firearms officer or the Registrar to issue, in accordance with such terms and conditions as the competent authority considers appropriate, an authorization, a licence or a registration certificate, as the case may be, to the person for sustenance or employment purposes.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 113(1) of the English version of the Act after paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the competent authority may, notwithstanding that the person is or will be subject to a prohibition order, make an order authorizing a chief firearms officer or the Registrar to issue, in accordance with such terms and conditions as the competent authority considers appropriate, an authorization, a licence or an inscription certificate, as the case may be, to the person for sustenance or employment purposes.

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an authorization, a licence or an inscription certificate may not be denied to the person in respect of whom the order was made solely on the basis of a prohibition order against the person or the commission of an offence in respect of which a prohibition order was made against the person; and
